alkylation reagent: alkylation with iodoacetamide (11 mM) at 25 °C for 15 min under dark condition.
alkylation concentration: alkylation with iodoacetamide (11 mM) at 25 °C for 15 min under dark condition.
cleavage agent: digested with trypsin at a mass ratio of 1:50 (trypsin: protein) for 12 h.
enrichment method: incubated with mono-, double-, and tri-methylated resin antibody beads at 4 °C to enrich the methylated peptides.
fractionation method: High pH reverse-phase HPLC with a C 18 column (5 μm particles, 10 mm i.d., 250 mm in length) was used to fractionate the tryptic peptides.
instrument: the eluted peptides were subjected to nanospray ionization and then MS/MS analysis in a Q Exactive Plus (Thermo) as previously described.
ionization type: the eluted peptides were subjected to nanospray ionization and then MS/MS analysis in a Q Exactive Plus.
labeling: the methylated substrates and methylation sites during tomato fruit ripening were identified by LC-MS/MS.
reduction reagent: The extracted proteins were reduced with dithiothreitol (5 mM) at 56 °C for 30 min.
reduction concentration: The extracted proteins were reduced with dithiothreitol (5 mM) at 56 °C for 30 min.
